content.assessment: A presumption-against-closure duty would be inserted into a governance structure where school maintenance authority sits with tankerületi centers, not municipalities, and where closure decisions are already made using efficiency/enrollment ratios rather than a demographic-decline-specific statutory procedure. The proposal does not itself alter who holds the closure decision, so its institutional effect depends entirely on whether the procedural duty binds the same central authority that currently applies enrollment thresholds. The 2026 reform, which restructures but preserves multi-tier centralized authority while adding some principal/municipal input, offers a plausible legislative vehicle, but this reform does not abolish the underlying authority structure the presumption would need to constrain.
opportunities: Could be formally attached to the 2026 governance reform's restructuring, embedding the safeguard within a body that already plans some devolution of input
strengths: Targets a genuine institutional gap: closure currently proceeds via administrative action without a dedicated statutory alternatives-assessment step
Aligns with the 2026 reform's direction of restoring some principal/municipal input, offering an existing legislative vehicle rather than requiring a wholly new one
threats: National class-size and per-pupil funding formulas create closure pressure independent of governance procedure; a documented-alternatives requirement does not remove this structural fiscal driver and so may leave the presumption formally satisfied but substantively inert
weaknesses: Does not change the underlying authority: tankerületi centers retain both maintenance authority and, per the 2026 reform, continued centralized multi-tier control, so the same body that applies closure criteria would also administer the presumption

Enrollment/efficiency-ratio criteria documented in 2022 Sopron-region closures could persist unchanged even after a documentation requirement is added, since the proposal targets process rather than the substantive class-size/funding thresholds driving closures
